message.content: Amazon Managed Grafanaを使ってみたいのですが AWS Identity Center以外のログイン方法について教えてください

addedBlocks:
## aws-mcp
MCP Proxy for AWS provides access to SigV4 protected MCP servers through a single interface. This proxy handles authentication and request routing to the appropriate backend services.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Explore Amazon Managed Grafana login methods

content.text:
Amazon Managed Grafana のログイン方法は、AWS IAM Identity Center 以外に以下の選択肢があります。

## SAML 2.0 認証

独自の IdP（Identity Provider）を使ってSSO連携できます。

**対応している主なIdP:**
- Okta
- Azure Active Directory
- OneLogin
- Google Workspace
- その他 SAML 2.0 対応のIdP全般

**設定の流れ:**
1. Grafana ワークスペース作成時に認証方法で「SAML」を選択
2. IdP から メタデータURL または メタデータXMLを取得
3. Grafana の Admin/Editor/Viewer ロールを SAML のアサーション属性にマッピング

## 比較表

| 方法 | 向いているケース |
|---|---|
| AWS IAM Identity Center | AWS 組織全体で統一管理したい |
| SAML 2.0 | 既存の社内IdP（Okta等）を使いたい / AWS Organizations 外のユーザーも含めたい |

## 注意点

- **両方同時に有効化**することも可能です（IAM Identity Center + SAML の併用）
- SAML を使う場合でも、Grafana ワークスペースへのアクセス権限（IAMレベル）は別途必要です
- ユーザーのロール（Admin/Editor/Viewer）は SAML アサーション内の属性値でマッピングします

---

CARTA グループで利用されている IdP が決まっていれば、その具体的な設定手順もご案内できます。何かご不明な点はありますか？

(CCO202603)

message.content: 関係するドキュメントについても教えてください

content.text: AWS のドキュメントを検索します。

input.search_phrase: Amazon Managed Grafana authentication SAML login methods

content.text:
{"content":{"result":[{"rank_order":1,"title":"Use SAML with your Amazon Managed Grafana workspace","context":"Amazon Managed Grafana supports SAML 2.0 authentication, allowing organizations to use third-party identity providers (such as Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin, CyberArk, and Ping Identity) for single sign-on to Grafana workspaces, rather than authenticating through IAM. The workspace acts as a service provider (SP) in the SAML flow, redirecting users to their IdP for authentication and then validating the returned SAML assertion to grant access. Administrators can map IdP groups and roles to Grafana teams and roles for fine-grained access control, and can configure how SAML assertion attributes (such as role, name, and other user information) are mapped within the Grafana workspace","url":"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/authentication-in-AMG-SAML.html"},{"rank_order":2,"title":"UpdateWorkspaceAuthentication","context":"The **UpdateWorkspaceAuthentication** API operation configures the identity provider (IdP) for an Amazon Managed Grafana workspace using SAML, allowing you to map SAML assertion attributes to user information and assign Admin/Editor roles based on group membership. It accepts authentication provider types (SAML or AWS SSO) and an optional SAML configuration via a POST request to a workspace-specific endpoint, returning the updated authentication description. Possible errors include `AccessDeniedException`, `ConflictException`, and other common errors, and changes may take a few minutes to take effect","url":"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/APIReference/API_UpdateWorkspaceAuthentication.html"},{"rank_order":3,"title":"ManagedGrafana: Update-MGRFWorkspaceAuthentication Cmdlet | AWS Tools for PowerShell","context":"The `Update-MGRFWorkspaceAuthentication` PowerShell cmdlet calls the Amazon Managed Grafana `UpdateWorkspaceAuthentication` API to configure the identity provider (IdP) that authenticates users for a workspace using SAML. It allows administrators to map SAML assertion attributes (such as email, name, login, groups, and roles) to workspace user information and assign Admin and Editor roles based on group membership. The cmdlet is available across multiple AWS PowerShell modules and accepts parameters for workspace ID, authentication providers, SAML configuration, IdP metadata, and role mappings","url":"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/powershell/v5/reference/items/Update-MGRFWorkspaceAuthentication.html"},{"rank_order":4,"title":"setting-up-ec2-instance-profiles","skill_description":"Configures EC2 instances to securely call AWS services by creating and attaching IAM roles via instance profiles, eliminating hardcoded credentials. Use when an EC2 instance needs permissions to access AWS services like S3, DynamoDB, SQS, or CloudWatch through temporary credentials.

Services: ec2, iam
Tasks: configure, secure
Persona: developer, devops
Workload: security","skill_name":"setting-up-ec2-instance-profiles"},{"rank_order":5,"title":"creating-secrets-using-best-practices","skill_description":"Creates and manages secrets in AWS Secrets Manager following security best practices. Always use this skill when creating secrets — it sets up dedicated KMS encryption keys, automatic rotation, least-privilege IAM policies, CloudTrail auditing, and lifecycle management that are essential for production-grade secret handling.

Services: secrets-manager, kms, iam, cloudtrail, cloudwatch, lambda
Tasks: create, secure, configure
Persona: developer, devops
Workload: security","skill_name":"creating-secrets-using-best-practices"},{"rank_order":6,"title":"aws-messaging-and-streaming","skill_description":"Guides use of AWS messaging and streaming services. Covers Amazon SQS, Amazon SNS, Amazon EventBridge, Amazon MQ, Amazon Kinesis Data Streams, Amazon Data Firehose, Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink, and Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka (MSK). Use when implementing messaging and streaming patterns. 


Services: sqs, sns, eventbridge, mq, kinesis, kds, firehose, flink, msf, msk, kafka
Tasks: learn, architect, debug, deploy
Persona: developer, architect, devops
Workload: serverless, event-driven, streaming, data-analytics","skill_name":"aws-messaging-and-streaming"}]}}

aiTitle: Explore Amazon Managed Grafana login methods

input.search_phrase: Amazon Managed Grafana workspace authentication user access IAM Identity Center

content.text:
{"content":{"result":[{"rank_order":1,"title":"Use AWS IAM Identity Center with your Amazon Managed Grafana workspace","context":"Amazon Managed Grafana integrates with AWS IAM Identity Center to enable identity federation, allowing users to sign in to Grafana workspaces using their existing corporate directory credentials while enforcing security policies like password rules and two-factor authentication. If no existing directory is available, IAM Identity Center provides a built-in user directory for creating users and groups, and note that IAM users/roles cannot be used for permissions within Grafana workspaces. Using IAM Identity Center requires AWS Organizations to be activated, and several IAM policies (such as AWSGrafanaAccountAdministrator, AWSSSODirectoryAdministrator, and AWSSSOMasterAccountAdministrator) are needed to properly administer workspaces depending on whether the account is standalone or part of an organization","url":"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/authentication-in-AMG-SSO.html"},{"rank_order":2,"title":"Authenticate users in Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces","context":"Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces authenticate users via single sign-on (SSO) through an organization's identity provider, rather than through IAM. Each workspace supports one or both of two authentication methods: SAML 2.0-compatible identity providers and AWS IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO). Users must also be explicitly granted permissions (user, editor, or administrator) to access functionality within a workspace","url":"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/authentication-in-AMG.html"},{"rank_order":3,"title":"Users","context":"In Amazon Managed Grafana, users are not added directly within the Grafana workspace. Instead, authentication is handled through IAM Identity Center or an identity provider, and access to workspaces is granted via the Amazon Managed Grafana console. For more details, users are directed to the documentation on managing user and group access to Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces","url":"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/Grafana-users.html"},{"rank_order":4,"title":"setting-up-ec2-instance-profiles","skill_description":"Configures EC2 instances to securely call AWS services by creating and attaching IAM roles via instance profiles, eliminating hardcoded credentials. Use when an EC2 instance needs permissions to access AWS services like S3, DynamoDB, SQS, or CloudWatch through temporary credentials.

Services: ec2, iam
Tasks: configure, secure
Persona: developer, devops
Workload: security","skill_name":"setting-up-ec2-instance-profiles"},{"rank_order":5,"title":"aws-iam","skill_description":"Verified corrections for IAM behaviors that AI agents frequently get wrong — policy evaluation edge cases, trust policy gotchas, STS session limits, Organizations quirks, and SAML/MFA specifics. Use alongside documentation when working with IAM roles, policies, STS, or Organizations. Do NOT use for non-IAM authorization like Cognito user-pool policies or app-level RBAC.

Services: iam, sts, organizations
Tasks: configure, secure, audit, debug
Persona: developer, security-engineer, devops
Workload: security","skill_name":"aws-iam"}]}}

content.text:
関連ドキュメントが見つかりました。

---

## Amazon Managed Grafana 認証関連ドキュメント

### 認証全般

- **[Authenticate users in Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/authentication-in-AMG.html)**
  認証方式の概要。SAML 2.0 と IAM Identity Center の両方について説明。まずここを読むと全体像がつかめます。

---

### SAML 2.0

- **[Use SAML with your Amazon Managed Grafana workspace](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/authentication-in-AMG-SAML.html)**
  SAMLの設定手順、IdPとのマッピング（ロール・グループ・ユーザー属性）の詳細。Okta / Azure AD / OneLogin / CyberArk / Ping Identity などの具体例も記載。

- **[UpdateWorkspaceAuthentication API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/APIReference/API_UpdateWorkspaceAuthentication.html)**
  SAMLやIAM Identity Centerの設定をAPIで行う場合のリファレンス。IaC（Terraform等）で自動化したい場合に参照。

---

### IAM Identity Center（参考）

- **[Use AWS IAM Identity Center with your Amazon Managed Grafana workspace](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/grafana/latest/userguide/authentication-in-AMG-SSO.html)**
  IAM Identity Center との連携手順。AWS Organizations の要否や必要なIAMポリシーについても説明。

---

### 補足

SAMLを選ぶ場合は **認証全般のページ** → **SAMLのページ** の順に読むのがスムーズです。既存のIdP（Okta等）がある場合はSAMLページに各IdP向けの具体的な設定例が載っています。

(CCO202603)

lastPrompt: 関係するドキュメントについても教えてください
